title: clarkia biloba subsp. brandegeei
intro: Clarkia biloba subsp. brandegeei, also known as the Brandegee's clarkia or farewell-to-spring, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the evening primrose family. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Clarkia biloba subsp. brandegeei include reducing swelling and promoting wound healing, as well as potentially lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
